{{Quote|I don't trust you, but it can't hurt to have you help me, because I am too short-handed. And of course if you betray me I will have you killed.|Commander Barrezz, to a spacer|<ref name="SWG:StS" />}}
'''Arlan Barrezz''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] in [[Imperial Intelligence/Legends|Imperial Intelligence]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]].

==Biography==
{{Quote|Well lucky for you, the Empire needs to find out what was in that cargo more than it needs to spend time punishing you for your crimes. Are you willing to help me to clear your name?|Commander Barrezz, to a spacer|<ref name="SWG:StS" />}}
After the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], Commander Arlan Barrezz was stationed in the [[Bestine (city)/Legends|Bestine]] [[Imperial garrison (Bestine)|Imperial garrison]] on [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]].<ref name="SWG:StS" /> His immediate superior was [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] [[Maximilian Vox]].<ref name="SWG:TN">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=class|Legacy|To Naboo|Tatooine}}</ref>

In [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]],<ref name="SWG_timeline">{{SWG_timeline}}</ref> Barrezz was tasked with the job of finding cargo, smuggled from the [[Tansarii Point Station/Legends|Tansarii Point Station]]. The cargo was now lost among the various denizens on Tatooine. Both the Empire and the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance]] were very interested in finding it, so time was of the essence. Fortunately, a [[Spacer/Legends|spacer]] chose seek out Barrezz and offer his assistance, instead of Rebel [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Carh'la Bastra]]. Barrezz agreed to use the spacer's help, so he immediately gave him work.<ref name="SWG:StS" />

Commander Barrezz first tasked the spacer with finding the inventory of the cargo content that was acquired by a [[Sand Crawler 345|group]] of [[Jawa/Legends|Jawa]] traders. The Jawas had recently been spotted at [[Tosche Station/Legends|Tosche Station]], near [[Anchorhead/Legends|Anchorhead]]. There, the spacer learnt from the manager [[Cuan Talon]] that he should ask his warehouse manager [[Janus]] but the latter had already been killed by [[Desert Demons]].<ref name="SWG:TS">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=class|Legacy|Tosche Station|Tatooine}}</ref> From Janus' datapd, the spacer recovered the first part of the cargo data and discovered that the Jawa had also visited the [[Lars homestead/Legends|Lars homestead]]. At the homestead, [[Zef Ando]] agreed to give the inventory of what he bought from the Jawas.<ref name="SWG:LH">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=other}}Legacy Quest: "Lars Homestead" on Tatooine</ref> The spacer finally reached the Jawa sandcrawler in the [[Western Dune Sea/Legends|Western Dune Sea]] but it was under attack from [[Tusken Raider/Legends|Tusken Raiders]]. The spacer attacked the Tusken Raiders and rescued the Jawas and the droid [[FA-2PO]]. The spacer later recovered Jawa encryption keys from the Tusken Raiders and finally accessed to the sandcrawler database.<ref name="SWG:JT">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=class|Legacy|Jawa Tracks|Tatooine}}</ref>

Barrezz then tasked the spacer with going to the [[Valarian Depot]] near Bestine. It turned out that the [[Valarian/Legends|Valarian]] crime syndicate purchased a portion of the lost cargo. The spacer was told to do a complete inventory, by checking the manifests on the cargo pallets. Though the job was highly dangerous, due to the number of guards patrolling the depot, the spacer succeeded.<ref name="SWG:VH">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=class|Legacy|Valarian Haul|Tatooine}}</ref>

Barrezz was pleased, but there was still more work to be done. Barrezz immediately tasked the spacer with going to the [[Darklighter family/Legends|Darklighter]] [[Darklighter cache|storage facility]] near their [[Darklighter estate|estate]]. It turned out that some of the cargo the Empire was looking for ended up in their hands, so the spacer needed to do another complete inventory of their cache. The spacer agreed to help, and immediately sought out the storage facility, which was actually a cave, overrun by Desert Demon looters. At the cave's entrance, was a guard named [[Draci]]. Draci informed the spacer that the cave's security system failed, and that he would need to move quickly. Fortunately, the spacer managed to do a complete inventory, before most of the cache was destroyed or stolen by the scavengers.<ref name="SWG:DC">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=class|Legacy|Darklighter Cache|Tatooine}}</ref>

Barrezz still had one final task for the spacer. The spacer now needed to deliver the data to Barrezz's slicer, [[Jem Lavar]]. Compared to the previous tasks, finding Jem Levar was a trivial matter, but the spacer had nonetheless to rescued Lavar from a group of [[Smuggler/Legends|gunrunners]]. Barrezz was thankful to the spacer for giving Levar the data. Jem Lavar analyzed the inventory data from the Jawas, the Darklighter and the Valarians and finally discovered that the [[R2-series astromech droid/Legends|R2 unit]] that both the Empire and the Rebellion were looking for was bought by the [[Junk dealer/Legends|junk dealer]] [[Watto/Legends|Watto]].<ref name="SWG:AotE">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=class|Legacy|Agent of the Empire|Tatooine}}</ref>

The spacer was tasked by Barrezz to retrieve the droids and thus headed to [[Wald's Parts|Watto's Shop]] in [[Mos Espa/Legends|Mos Espa]]. There, the spacer learnt that the droids was already sold in spare parts. Nevertheless, Watto agreed to re-assemble the droid if the spacer was able to get all the parts.<ref name="SWG:WB">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=class|Legacy|Watto's Bargain|Tatooine}}</ref> The spacer obtained the droid body from [[Ji'Ni Sil]] in the [[Jawa mountain fortress/Legends|Jawa Mountain Fortress]],<ref name="SWG:BB">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=other}}Legacy Quest: "Body Building" on Tatooine</ref> the legs from [[Syndil Na'Marr]] and a [[Bothan/Legends|Bothan]] pilot,<ref name="SWG:LW">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=other}}Legacy Quest: "Leg Work" on Tatooine</ref> the [[Motivator/Legends|motivator]] from one of [[An'Kwee]]'s death [[Podracing/Legends|podracer]] pilot friend,<ref name="SWG:PR">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=other}}Legacy Quest: "Pod Retrieval" on Tatooine</ref> the motivator booster controller from [[Hilbrun|Pah]] and his gang,<ref name="SWG:RM">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=other}}Legacy Quest: "Repo Man" on Tatooine</ref> the diagnostic controller in the [[Squill Cave]],<ref name="SWG:SD">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=other}}Legacy Quest: "Squill Diving" on Tatooine</ref> and finally the head from [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba the Hutt's]] [[Supervisor droid/Legends|supervisor droid]] [[EV-9D9/Legends|EV-9D9]].<ref name="SWG:TtH">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=other}}Legacy Quest: "Tracking the Head" on Tatooine</ref><ref name="SWG:R">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=other}}Legacy Quest: "Runaways" on Tatooine</ref> When the spacer returned to the shop with all parts, Watto finally helped re-assemble the droid.<ref name="SWG:PiT">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=class|Legacy|Putting it Together|Tatooine}}</ref> From the reassembled droid, the spacer was able to recover the information both the Empire and the Rebellion were looking for. Arlan Barrezz ordered the spacer to immediately transmit the data to Lieutenant Vox on [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]].<ref name="SWG:TN" />

==Behind the scenes==
Arlan Barrezz was a [[Wikipedia:Non-player character|non-player character]] in the [[2003]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars Galaxies]]'', a [[Wikipedia:Massively multiplayer online role-playing game|massively multiplayer online roleplaying game]] developed by [[Sony]] Online Entertainment and published by [[LucasArts]],<ref name="SWG:StS" /> prior to its closure on [[December 15]], [[2011]].<ref name="Shutdown">{{SonyCite|url=playerinfo|text=IMPORTANT INFORMATION ABOUT ''STAR WARS'' GALAXIES™}}</ref> Arlan Barrezz was added to the game as part of the "[[W:c:swg:Legacy Quest|Legacy Quest]]" with the release of the "[[W:c:swg:Publish 25 Notes for November 15, 2005|Publish 25]]," a.k.a. the "[[W:c:swg:New Game Enhancements|New Game Enhancements]]," on [[November 15]], [[2005]].<ref name="SonyCite-Publish Archive 2005">{{SonyCite|url=players/content.vm?id=64886&resource=publish|text=Publish Archive 2005}}</ref> In the game, Barrezz is part of a long, epic quest, referred to as the "Legacy Quest." The player can choose to help either Barrezz, or a Rebel [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] named [[Carh'la Bastra]] with finding the lost cargo.<ref name="SWG:StS" />

In [[2008]]'s ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'', Barrezz's name is misspelled as "Arlen Barezz."<ref name="TCSWE" />
